items.title: Pirates' Skenes loses no-hit bid in 7th but gets w...
items.summary: Pirates ace Paul Skenes had his no-hit bid vs. the Rockies end in the seventh inning Tuesday night by Mickey Moniak's clean single.

items.title: Raleigh ends drought with first hit since April 27...
items.summary: Cal Raleigh's hitless streak was the longest in the majors this season, and his knock in the seventh inning was his first since April 27.

items.title: Alvarez's knee injury dampens Mets' blowout win
items.summary: Mets catcher Francisco Alvarez is scheduled to undergo an MRI after leaving Tuesday's 10-2 win with a right knee injury.
items.author: Jorge Castillo

items.title: Dodgers lose but see Ohtani break slump with HR
items.summary: Shohei Ohtani snapped an 11-game homerless drought -- tied for his longest as a Dodger -- as part of a night in which he also singled, drew a walk and was robbed of another hit to break out of a prolonged slump Tuesday night in a loss to the Giants.
items.author: Alden Gonzalez

items.title: Sources: MLB, players' union hold first CBA talks
items.summary: Top officials from Major League Baseball and the MLB Players Association met Tuesday to officially open up collective bargaining talks a little more than six months before the expiration of the game's labor deal, sources told ESPN.
items.author: Jeff Passan

items.title: Rangers' Nimmo day-to-day with sprained ankle
items.summary: Rangers outfielder Brandon Nimmo left Tuesday night's game in the sixth inning with a sprained left ankle and is considered day-to-day.

items.title: Sources: Volpe returns to Yanks; Caballero to IL
items.summary: Anthony Volpe is returning to the Yankees, who recalled the former top shortstop prospect from the minor leagues Tuesday.

items.title: Braves put Murphy on IL, could miss 8 weeks
items.summary: The Braves placed catcher Sean Murphy on the injured list with a fractured left middle finger, and manager Walt Weiss said he could be out up to eight weeks.
